It just makes sense. A more a higher ticket sales, a more involved sale generally needs more education and just more communication. So in any case, most pre launch periods, nine to 12 days. And then the open cart period is typically five to seven days. Now you can go shorter. I've had open cart periods for my launches that were like a day and a half. Okay. And then and the thing is, is if you're going to go really short, probably not the good thing to do on your very first launch, because if something doesn't go perfect, it doesn't give you time to recover. So a longer cart period gives you more time to just to tweak your message and recover from any mistakes you make during your open cart. Remember, there's that product launch parachute. The product launch parachute means that there's lots and lots of interactions throughout this entire process. So if something isn't perfect, you have time to recover. You're not depending on one email or one video or one anything. So those really short open cart periods, they're a little on the dangerous side. ## [09:09 - 10:02]

because of the nature of the way I teach product launch formula. But just because I do that doesn't mean you have to do it.
